tasks.description: Add logActivity() calls to POST/PATCH/DELETE handlers for core canvas + journey + share API routes (workspaces, tabs, sections, steps, connections, stages, touchpoints, touchpoint-connections, shares)
acceptance_criteria: Every POST handler in owned routes calls logActivity() with action='created' after successful insert
Every PATCH handler in owned routes calls logActivity() with action='updated' after successful update, with details containing changed field names
Every DELETE handler in owned routes calls logActivity() with action='deleted' after successful delete, using entity name snapshot
logActivity() is fire-and-forget — do NOT await in the response chain or block the response on it
For DELETE routes that don't already use .select().single(), add it to capture entity_name before deletion
All logActivity() calls include workspace_id, user_id (from user.id), entity_type (table name), entity_id, entity_name

patterns_to_follow: logActivity signature: { supabase, workspace_id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, entity_name, details? }
For POST: call after successful insert, use returned data.id and data.name (or body.name)
For PATCH: call after successful update, use returned data. Include { changed_fields: Object.keys(updates) } in details

For DELETE: modify to .delete().eq('id', id).select().single() if needed, use returned data for entity_name. If entity has no name field (connections, touchpoint-connections), use entity_type as entity_name
Fire-and-forget pattern: call logActivity() without awaiting in the response path. The function handles its own errors internally
For shares routes: use action='shared' for POST instead of 'created'
workspace_id source: POST routes get it from body.workspace_id or body; PATCH/DELETE routes get it from the returned entity data
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — logActivity is server-side only, no envelope concern
Supabase RLS silent mutations — routes with PGRST116 handling already check for empty results

tasks.special_instructions: This is a formulaic task: add the same pattern (import + logActivity call) to every POST/PATCH/DELETE handler. For entities without a 'name' field (connections, touchpoint-connections), use a descriptive string like 'Connection' or the source/target step names if available. Do NOT modify GET handlers. Do NOT modify the logActivity utility itself.

tasks.description: Add logActivity() calls to POST/PATCH/DELETE handlers for org/overlay API routes (teams, roles, people, tools, step-roles, perspectives, annotations, comments, tasks, runbooks, runbook-steps) + IMP-016 playbook button on read-only runbooks
acceptance_criteria: Every POST handler in owned API routes calls logActivity() with action='created' after successful insert
Every PATCH handler in owned API routes calls logActivity() with action='updated' after successful update, with details containing changed field names
Every DELETE handler in owned API routes calls logActivity() with action='deleted' after successful delete, using entity name snapshot
Comments POST handler uses action='commented' instead of 'created'
Runbook PATCH to status='completed' uses action='completed' instead of 'updated'
logActivity() is fire-and-forget — do NOT await in the response chain
For DELETE routes that don't already use .select().single(), add it to capture entity_name
IMP-016: Playbook button is visible on completed/cancelled runbooks (read-only mode) in runbook-view.tsx

patterns_to_follow: logActivity signature: { supabase, workspace_id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, entity_name, details? }
For comments: use action='commented' on POST
For runbooks: use action='completed' on PATCH when status changes to 'completed'
For runbook-steps: use action='updated' on PATCH, include { status: body.status } in details if status changed
For entities without 'name' field (step-roles, annotations), use entity_type or a descriptive label as entity_name
Fire-and-forget: call logActivity() without awaiting in response path

IMP-016: In runbook-view.tsx, the Playbook Link button is currently behind an !isReadOnly guard. Remove or adjust the guard so the button shows for read-only runbooks too. The playbook-view.tsx already handles isReadOnly correctly.
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — logActivity is server-side only
Supabase RLS silent mutations — PGRST116 handling already exists in some routes

tasks.special_instructions: Formulaic task: add import + logActivity call to every POST/PATCH/DELETE handler. Special actions: comments POST='commented', runbooks PATCH to completed='completed'. For IMP-016: find the Playbook Link button in runbook-view.tsx and ensure it renders even when isReadOnly is true. The button should be visible for completed/cancelled runbooks. Do NOT modify GET handlers. Do NOT modify the logActivity utility itself.

shared_files.note: Both slots read activity.ts for the logActivity import. Neither modifies it.
